canonical_question: How do I design an API gateway?
aliases: build an API gateway for microservices
Kong vs NGINX vs Envoy gateway comparison
API gateway rate limiting authentication routing
constraints: Never put business logic in the gateway — it must remain a thin routing and cross-cutting concerns layer
Always deploy at least two gateway instances behind a load balancer
Rate limiting and authentication must be enforced at the gateway, not delegated to individual services
Do not chain multiple gateway layers (hardware LB + software proxy + API gateway)
TLS termination should happen at the gateway edge

skip_this_unit_if.condition: Application is a simple monolith with a single backend and no microservices
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Use a standard reverse proxy (NGINX/Caddy) instead of an API gateway

skip_this_unit_if.condition: User needs service mesh communication patterns between internal services
skip_this_unit_if.use_instead: Service mesh (Istio/Linkerd) handles east-west traffic; API gateways handle north-south

inputs_needed.question: What is your expected concurrent request volume?
What protocols do your services use?
